description: Test cases for the requirement-consistency-check skill. Each eval runs a realistic user prompt against an agent with the skill loaded; the skill must emit a CLARIFY block with the correct Status and the expected blocker classes. Derived from real DD-1031 rework. Assertions are prose for iteration 1 — tighten after first run.

evals.name: fragmented-scope-no-dod
evals.prompt: valida que el provider mutua quede bien y de paso revisa los hardcodes que falten
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block, Status: BLOCKED. C1 blocker (no DoD / acceptance oracle: '¿qué define "bien"? ¿hay CSV oracle v1 y dónde?') and C7 blocker for 'los hardcodes que falten' (which files / which channel). No REQ written.

evals.name: reversible-decision-no-value
evals.prompt: externaliza todos los diccionarios hardcodeados del provider mutua a tablas en el seed
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block, Status: BLOCKED. C2 blocker: structural change (new tables/pattern) with no stated concrete value; question must surface rule 05 §2 (immutable protocol vocab = acceptable hardcode), rule 18 YAGNI, and 'no peer provider externalizes this' — ask the user to confirm the value/trade-off before implementing. C6 (contradiction vs rule 05 §2) also flagged.

evals.name: migration-missing-v1-trace
evals.prompt: migra el canal API de mutua igual que asitur, que los ACTUALIZACION se integren
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block, Status: BLOCKED. C3 blocker: no v1 behavior trace — must ask for the v1 source path and the missing-datum branch per tipo (does v1 omit-as-success, error, or skip when no caseNotificationNumber?). Reference: not tracing this caused E1–E13.

evals.name: work-already-exists
evals.prompt: necesito que migres los cases v1 de mutua de mysql a postgres como hace linea directa
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block. C4 blocker: must audit the repo first (provider_migrations/mutuamadrilena.py already exists and mirrors LD) and ask the user whether to scope only the delta vs re-plan. Should NOT plan a from-scratch migration.

evals.name: channel-data-shape-assumption
evals.prompt: resuelve el contrato de mutua por código postal en el canal API igual que en email
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block, Status: BLOCKED. C5 blocker: the API payload may not carry a postal code (it doesn't — only empresa/ramo/anio/codSiniestro/idServicio); ask for a real API sample before designing ZIP→contract for that channel.

evals.name: ambiguous-encrypt-which-key
evals.prompt: encripta las credenciales que subimos de mutua
evals.expected_output: CLARIFY block. C7 blocker: which fields (password/api_token vs username/client_id), which encryption key (local vs committed-seed vs prod), which env — must ask before encrypting so the ciphertext is valid for the target.

evals.name: clean-requirement-passes-through
evals.prompt: Añade un índice en cases.guai_case_id; columna ya existe, sólo el índice CONCURRENTLY, sin cambio de lógica. DoD: migración Alembic + make test verde.
evals.expected_output: Status: CLEAN (one line). No blockers; bounded scope, explicit DoD, no v1/channel/decision ambiguity. Continues straight to analyze-requirement.

evals.name: mid-cycle-scope-expansion
evals.prompt: [mid-cycle, REQ already approved for the externalization] ahora también encripta las credenciales y corre la migración mysql->postgres
evals.expected_output: Skill re-runs on the DELTA only. CLARIFY block with new C7 (encryption key/scope) + C4 (migration may already exist) blockers; gate re-opens — no further implementation until resolved. Must NOT silently fold the new asks into the in-flight REQ.
